steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $x^{3}(8x^{2}-5x+11)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content: The terms $8x^5$, $-5x^4$, and $11x^3$ share no common numerical factor (GCD of 8, 5, 11 is 1). The smallest power of $x$ present is $x^3$, so the GCF is $x^3$.

steps.title: Factor out $x^3$
steps.content:
Divide each term by $x^3$:
$$8x^5 - 5x^4 + 11x^3 = x^3(8x^2 - 5x + 11)$$
Check by expanding: $x^3 \cdot 8x^2 = 8x^5$, $x^3 \cdot (-5x) = -5x^4$, $x^3 \cdot 11 = 11x^3$. Correct.

steps.title: Evaluate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A. $x^4(8x-5) = 8x^5 - 5x^4$ — missing the $11x^3$ term; eliminated.
  • B. $x^3(8x^2-5x+11) = 8x^5 - 5x^4 + 11x^3$ — matches.
  • C. $11x^3(8x^2-5x+1) = 88x^5 - 55x^4 + 11x^3$ — wrong coefficients; eliminated.
  • D. $5x^5(-5x^4+11x^3+1)$ produces $x^9$ terms; eliminated.
That confirms B is correct.
